---
title: "UBCO Freshwater Science"
subtitle: "Course pathways, designations, and learning outcomes"
date: last-modified
---

# A planning resource for current and prospective students

Welcome to the planning hub for the **Freshwater Science** undergraduate
program at the [UBC Okanagan campus](https://ok.ubc.ca/). This site
helps you:

- **Browse the courses** that count toward Freshwater Science, with
  current-year prerequisites, equivalencies, and offering status.
- **Plan a path** to the **PAg** (BC Institute of Agrologists) or
  **RPBio** (College of Applied Biology) professional designation
  alongside your degree.
- **Track learning outcomes** at the program, department, and
  individual course level.
- **See where each skill is scaffolded** across the four-year arc
  (from "intro to R" to "designing a field study").
- **Use the interactive planner** to test course combinations against
  your degree requirements and designation goals.

## Quick starts

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-md-6 .g-col-12}

### I'm a current student
- Use the [Planner](https://example.shinyapps.io/ubco-freshwater-planner/)
  to map remaining terms.
- Check the [PAg pathway](designations/pag.qmd) and [RPBio
  pathway](designations/rpbio.qmd) to see what gaps you still need
  to fill before graduation.
- Browse the [course catalogue](courses/index.qmd) for prerequisites
  and "is this offered next year?" signals.

:::

::: {.g-col-md-6 .g-col-12}

### I'm a prospective student
- Skim the [program description](#about-the-program) below.
- Compare the [designation pathways](designations/pag.qmd) to see what
  professional qualifications this degree can lead to.
- Browse the [first-year courses](courses/index.qmd#year-1) to get a
  feel for the workload.

:::

:::

## About the program

The Freshwater Science program integrates aquatic biology, chemistry,
geography, and earth and environmental sciences. Students study water
quality and quantity, aquatic organisms, and the health of aquatic
ecosystems.

The four-year B.Sc. requires 120 credits with at least 78 in Science,
at least 42 at the upper level (300+), and at least 36 of those
upper-level credits in Science. The Honours track adds a research
thesis (EESC_O 449).

::: {.callout-note}
## Source of truth
Course descriptions and prerequisites are pulled daily from the
[UBC Okanagan Academic Calendar](https://okanagan.calendar.ubc.ca/).
Designation requirements are curated from the
[BCIA UBCO Approved Course List](https://www.bcia.com/) and the
[CAB Credentialing Standard](https://cab-bc.org/) by the program lead.
The [Methodology page](docs/methodology.qmd) details the data
pipeline and refresh cadence.
:::

## Who maintains this

Built and maintained by [Limnology Research Corp](https://limnology.ca)
on behalf of the program lead, Nelson R. Jatel, PAg. Source code at
[github.com/DrNelsonJatel/UBCO_Freshwater_Science_Program](https://github.com/DrNelsonJatel/UBCO_Freshwater_Science_Program).
Issues and suggestions welcome.
